title: Calophyllum schefferi
taxonomy: Calophyllum schefferi is a species of plant in the family Calophyllaceae. It is a member of the order Malpighiales and the clade Rosidae. The plant is part of the subclass Rosidae and the clade Pentapetalae. The family Calophyllaceae is a small family of flowering plants. Calophyllum schefferi's classification reflects its evolutionary relationships to other plants within the clade.
uses: Calophyllum schefferi is a plant species used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its roots and bark are often employed to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant's oil is also used to treat wounds and skin irritations. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of Calophyllum schefferi's medicinal uses.
benefits: Calophyllum schefferi, a plant native to the tropical regions of Asia, is believed to possess various health benefits. parts: Calophyllum schefferi has traditionally been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The bark, leaves, and seeds of the plant are used in traditional medicine. preparations: Calophyllum schefferi is a plant species used in herbal medicine, particularly in traditional Indonesian medicine. intro: Calophyllum schefferi, also known as the Scheffer's calophyllum, is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The health benefits of this plant include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Calophyllum schefferi include calophyllic acid, calophyllumol, and other flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to promote wound healing and skin health.
